one_time_description: Trust account claim settlement with the Government of Canada ratified December 13, 2021, paid February 28, 2022
economic_notes: Nation operates multiple government business enterprises including gas bar, contracting, construction, commercial development, building solutions, HDD, asphalt and concrete, investment holdings, and valve and supply. GBEs had combined revenue of $7,846,989 and net income of $331,771 in 2022. Nation also operates forestry, farm enterprises, and industry relations programs.
notes: Fiscal year 2022 (not 2024). Significant one-time trust account claim settlement of $7,470,269 from Government of Canada. Restatement of prior year figures due to change in accounting for Alexander First Nation Business Trust (added $1,186,632 to opening accumulated surplus). Change in consolidation: Alexander Industry Relations Corporation was dissolved and absorbed into the Nation during the year, adding $694,272 to accumulated surplus. 4 fully consolidated subsidiaries and 9 GBEs accounted for on modified equity basis. No schedule of remuneration provided in the extracted text. Total government transfers estimated as ISC ($27,893,941) + Health Services Canada ($6,419,506 per deferred revenue note) + Province of Alberta ($2,997,940) + CMHC ($1,665,480) + Service Canada ($591,474) = approximately $34,554,455 but note deferred revenue adjustments complicate this calculation. OSR calculated as total revenue $36,580,024 minus estimated government transfers $34,554,455.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
High ISC dependency (76.25%) — operations heavily reliant on federal transfers
